results.comment_text:
@user1:
Nit: would be nice to add `atol=1e-14` --- this checks for equality in the floating-point, so let's make it more explicit. Only worth doing if there are other changed to lump it to.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_uses_explicit_atol_1e_14__a1():
    """
    The review comment requests making floating-point equality explicit by adding
    `atol=1e-14` to the assert_allclose in the new RegularGridInterpolator
    test_nonscalar_values.

    This test inspects the source to ensure that specific assert_allclose call
    includes the explicit `atol=1e-14`.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('class\\s+TestRegularGridInterpolator\\b.*?def\\s+test_nonscalar_values\\s*\\(\\s*self\\s*,\\s*method\\s*\\)\\s*:(?P<body>.*?)(?:\\n\\s*def\\s|\\nclass\\s|\\Z)', source, flags=re.S)
    assert m is not None, 'Could not locate TestRegularGridInterpolator.test_nonscalar_values in /workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py; test layout changed.'

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_uses_explicit_atol_1e_14__a2():
    """
    The review comment requests making floating-point equality explicit by adding
    `atol=1e-14` to the assert_allclose in the new RegularGridInterpolator
    test_nonscalar_values.

    This test inspects the source to ensure that specific assert_allclose call
    includes the explicit `atol=1e-14`.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('class\\s+TestRegularGridInterpolator\\b.*?def\\s+test_nonscalar_values\\s*\\(\\s*self\\s*,\\s*method\\s*\\)\\s*:(?P<body>.*?)(?:\\n\\s*def\\s|\\nclass\\s|\\Z)', source, flags=re.S)
    body = m.group('body')
    call = re.search('assert_allclose\\s*\\(\\s*v\\s*,\\s*v2\\s*,(?P<args>[^)]*)\\)', body, flags=re.S)
    assert call is not None, 'Expected an assert_allclose(v, v2, ...) call in TestRegularGridInterpolator.test_nonscalar_values.'

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_uses_explicit_atol_1e_14__a3():
    """
    The review comment requests making floating-point equality explicit by adding
    `atol=1e-14` to the assert_allclose in the new RegularGridInterpolator
    test_nonscalar_values.

    This test inspects the source to ensure that specific assert_allclose call
    includes the explicit `atol=1e-14`.
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('class\\s+TestRegularGridInterpolator\\b.*?def\\s+test_nonscalar_values\\s*\\(\\s*self\\s*,\\s*method\\s*\\)\\s*:(?P<body>.*?)(?:\\n\\s*def\\s|\\nclass\\s|\\Z)', source, flags=re.S)
    body = m.group('body')
    call = re.search('assert_allclose\\s*\\(\\s*v\\s*,\\s*v2\\s*,(?P<args>[^)]*)\\)', body, flags=re.S)
    args = call.group('args')
    assert re.search('\\batol\\s*=\\s*1e-14\\b', args) is not None, f'Floating-point comparison in TestRegularGridInterpolator.test_nonscalar_values must specify `atol=1e-14` explicitly (per review comment). Found assert_allclose(v, v2, {args.strip()}) without that atol.'

# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_2_loops_over_all_trailing_entries__a1():
    """
    The review requested restructuring `test_nonscalar_values_2` to cover *all*
    entries across two trailing dimensions (e.g. 2*3=6), not just the last dim.

    We enforce this by source inspection:
    - the test must be restricted to num_trailing_dims = 2
    - and it must contain a nested loop over the last two axes
      (i in range(values.shape[-2]) and j in range(values.shape[-1]))
      with indexing values[..., i, j]
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'pytest.mark.parametrize("num_trailing_dims"' not in source, 'Expected test_nonscalar_values_2 to be restructured to fixed num_trailing_dims=2 (not parametrized over a range), per review comment.'

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_2_loops_over_all_trailing_entries__a2():
    """
    The review requested restructuring `test_nonscalar_values_2` to cover *all*
    entries across two trailing dimensions (e.g. 2*3=6), not just the last dim.

    We enforce this by source inspection:
    - the test must be restricted to num_trailing_dims = 2
    - and it must contain a nested loop over the last two axes
      (i in range(values.shape[-2]) and j in range(values.shape[-1]))
      with indexing values[..., i, j]
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert re.search('\\bnum_trailing_dims\\s*=\\s*2\\b', source), 'Expected test_nonscalar_values_2 to set num_trailing_dims=2 explicitly.'

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_2_loops_over_all_trailing_entries__a3():
    """
    The review requested restructuring `test_nonscalar_values_2` to cover *all*
    entries across two trailing dimensions (e.g. 2*3=6), not just the last dim.

    We enforce this by source inspection:
    - the test must be restricted to num_trailing_dims = 2
    - and it must contain a nested loop over the last two axes
      (i in range(values.shape[-2]) and j in range(values.shape[-1]))
      with indexing values[..., i, j]
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'for i in range(values.shape[-2])' in source and 'for j in range(values.shape[-1])' in source, 'Expected nested loops over both trailing dimensions in test_nonscalar_values_2 (i over values.shape[-2], j over values.shape[-1]) to cover all trailing entries.'

def test_rgi_nonscalar_values_2_loops_over_all_trailing_entries__a4():
    """
    The review requested restructuring `test_nonscalar_values_2` to cover *all*
    entries across two trailing dimensions (e.g. 2*3=6), not just the last dim.

    We enforce this by source inspection:
    - the test must be restricted to num_trailing_dims = 2
    - and it must contain a nested loop over the last two axes
      (i in range(values.shape[-2]) and j in range(values.shape[-1]))
      with indexing values[..., i, j]
    """
    source = open('/workspace/scipy/interpolate/tests/test_rgi.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'values[..., i, j]' in source, 'Expected indexing values[..., i, j] inside the nested loops to test all entries across the two trailing dimensions.'
